description: Same V2 engine as v2-evidence-status-fallback-guard, with one fix: src/hpi.py's HPI CSV parser now correctly reads the Land Registry HPI download instead of silently failing. This is a VALUATION-CHANGING fix — comparable prices are now adjusted using real regional House Price Index data instead of the flat 3%/year fallback that every valuation had silently been using.
changes_in_this_version: hpi.py _fetch_hpi_csv(): the parser checked for a 'Date' column that does not exist in the actual CSV (which has 'Period' and 'Pivotable date' instead) — every fetch silently returned None, and adjust_price_to_current() always fell back to flat 3%/year growth. Now uses 'Pivotable date' (falls back to 'Period' if absent).

hpi.py get_hpi_diagnostics(region): new read-only diagnostic function reporting whether real HPI data or the flat-rate fallback was used, and the latest HPI month available. Not consulted by any valuation calculation.
The flat-rate fallback (_adjust_with_flat_rate) is unchanged and still used automatically if the HPI fetch fails for any reason.
No changes to comparable selection, evidence weighting, confidence scoring, PDF generation, app UI, or the explanation engine.

source_files.note: Only change from parent version: MODEL_VERSION constant bumped.
Parser fix + diagnostics. This is the file that actually changes valuation output in this version.

known_limitations: This project is not a git repository, so this manifest + file snapshot is the only version record.

Recency-dependent fields (age_days) are still computed against datetime.now() at fetch time — unchanged from the parent baseline. See that baseline's manifest for the recency-drift caveat, which still applies.

The postcode discrepancy against project memory 'test-properties' noted in the parent baseline's manifest has not been resolved here either — the same session-consistent postcodes are used for comparability.
